inline_comments.message: I think you should keep the readiness image bump separate from this patch...

If I use "onap" as my namespace, it will be app.onap-onap?
Did you consider removing the "onap-" prefix and just use a namespace variable...
please remove and default to blank.

I know from past experience that if you use a name other than "mariadb" for the service, the mso JBoss server will not be able to contact the database.  There is config for SO and probably other apps that currently do not have a config map that use the original service names to resolve the db hosts.

If you check jboss logs, you should see a ton of JDBC errors and it is because it is trying to connect using "mariadb" and the DNS entry is now called "mso-mariadb"

once you run the createConfig script a namespace named "onap" is created, so if you run oneClick, it immediately fails.
once we get rid of the config image, or move to parent helm chart it will not be an issue
also if you try to run the script twice it also fails from the same reason. if you delete the namespace now, it will delete the components you already deployed

I think if the namespace exists, the script should not fail
Check whether needs to be prefixed

all consul configuration files are also mapped using configMap so i think we can use tpl here to replace namespace..

Thanks,
Mayank gupta
why do we have to fork all those? We should identify the why, and open a ticket in APP-C so they can make things configurable enough for us not to fork.
same as previous. Let's identify why we need to fork, and track this is APP-C ticket.

So basically, in the config we have `onap-namespace` placeholder that we want to replace with the appropriate namespace?
If so, let's use a real placeholder like {{ ONAP_NAMESPACE }} to remove any confusion.

Oh, is this like a placeholder, and the sed in the config-init will replace this with the appropriate value? If so, let's identify this as a real placeholder, something like {{ ONAP_NAMESPACE }} to remove any confusion.

Same applies to all the files after, if you agree.
how about we identify all the service with a log- prefix, same for the dep. And actually, same for any app. That way, it's easy to find component related objects.
why do we need this now?
Please change this in a different patch
should be done in a seperated patch
those things seems to be outside of this patch scope.
Can't be changed. tried, then appc tries to connect to appc-appc-sdnctldb01.
This is not namespace, but service.
Can't be changed. tried, then appc tries to connect to appc-appc-sdnctldb01.

In all those files there is dbhost, which should indeed be configurable. Let's open a ticket to APPC. Meanwhile, let's keep this in order to proceed (it was not easy to stabilize appc with changed dbhost).
same comment. Let's proceed meanwhile, until appc change.

Will change it to onap-namespace-placeholder, ok?
Wouldn't care to deal with '{{}}' in pattern replacement...
I will change this when consul config adjustment will be merged.

This is a large work, because different services communicate via service names.

Anyway during charts standardization the services names will change, therefore I would prefer not to do duplicate work.
I would like to push this commit through, since it would impact work on charts standardization.
WDYT?

On some versions of k8s, pod cannot resolve itself. That is why I changed. Was hard to find and understand this k8s behavior.
Would prefer to keep it unless it breaks something.
I changed mariadb to be mso-mariadb, this file has mariadb hardcoded.
ok, will add a patch to advance mso version.
Otherwise yaml parser fails on tpl. It thinks this is some config value and doesn't find it.
this pod didn't start, didn't find image. looked for a similar image, hope that I found the right one.

In Config Segregation of Robot, nsPrefix was introduced. Would you like to take up that as well in consideration
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/28423/3/kubernetes/robot/resources/config/eteshare/config/vm_properties.py
What I meant is add the namesapce to this. appc-dbhost.{{Values.nsPrefix}}
